note: Values are representative educational approximations based on publicly available data.

engines.name: Merlin 1D (Sea Level)
engines.description: Primary first-stage engine. Nine engines per Falcon 9 first stage. Gimbal-capable for TVC.

educational_notes.isp_discussion: SL Isp of 282 s vs vacuum 311 s demonstrates nozzle expansion losses at sea level.
educational_notes.throttle_discussion: Throttle-down to ~72% before Max-Q reduces aerodynamic loads.

engines.name: Merlin 1D Vacuum
engines.description: Vacuum-optimised second-stage engine. Extended nozzle for maximum Isp in vacuum.

educational_notes.isp_discussion: Vacuum Isp of 380 s (Block 5 upgrade) achieved by expanding the nozzle from ratio 16 (SL) to 165 (vac).
educational_notes.design_trade: Larger nozzle adds mass but the Isp gain far outweighs it for upper stages.

engines.name: Merlin 1D (Reusable Config)
engines.description: Merlin 1D configured for booster recovery (landing legs, grid fins, residual propellant reserve).

educational_notes.reuse_discussion: ~5500 kg penalty (legs, grid fins, propellant reserve) reduces LEO payload by ~5500 kg vs expendable.
